content:
# Surface Wave

**Summary**: Seismic waves that travel along the surface of the Earth.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009]] analyzed the impact of surface wave energy scattered by near-surface diffractors.

- [[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018]] applied SWI to update near-surface velocities for the first 200m of the model.

## Scientific Context

- [[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009]] Surface waves are often used in seismic exploration but can be complicated by near-surface scattering.

- [[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018]] applied SWI to update near-surface velocities for the first 200m of the model.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009]] Analyzed using a range of trial velocities to find the best fit.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The mapping algorithm requires a known or estimated velocity (V) for these waves to calculate the correct hyperbola. ([[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009, p. 19]])


- SWI provided more accurate velocity at the top 200m, leading to better definition of limestone velocity and improved RTM images. ([[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018, p. 62]])
